Course Title: Connected Vehicles and Expanding Attack Surfaces
Category: Transportation & Logistics: Aviation, Maritime, Logistics, Shipping, Automotive
Who is this for: Fleet managers, logistics coordinators, transport operations staff, automotive technicians, aviation maintenance personnel, maritime operations officers, supply chain managers, vehicle procurement specialists
Team or Dept this is for: Transportation, Logistics, Operations, Fleet Management, Supply Chain, Maintenance, Procurement
Description: IoT and AI increasing exposure in transport ecosystems.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with networking concepts and a general interest in how modern vehicles and transport systems connect to digital infrastructure are all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key vulnerabilities introduced by IoT and AI technologies across connected vehicle ecosystems and explain how expanding attack surfaces affect security in automotive, aviation, and maritime transport. You will also apply foundational cybersecurity principles to recognise potential threats within transport and logistics networks, helping you to better understand how organisations can begin to protect their connected infrastructure.
